item.title: 我应该先学习什么编程语言？ | What programming language should I learn first?
item.description:
您是否感到不知所措，不知道应该先学习哪种编程语言？ 好吧，你来对地方了。

在这篇文章中，我们将列出一些最流行的编程语言，并解释你可以用它们做什么。 这篇文章的目的是帮助您选择一种语言并开始编码。

学习编程概念比学习任何特定的编程语言重要得多。 那是因为这些概念独立于编程语言。 如果您想了解这方面的介绍，请随时下载我们的电子书。

让我们从列表开始。

注意：为了便于比较，所有代码示例都代表了用不同语言编写的大致相同的程序。

它有大量的库，可用于行业的各个分支。 它在 QA 自动化任务中也非常受欢迎。

这是一种非常容易学习的语言：它使用缩进而不是花括号，并且不需要使用分号。 由于它易于学习，因此它通常是非程序员（如科学家、机械工程师、会计师等）的首选语言。

它看起来像什么？

 import json data = json . loads ( '{ "foo": 1, "bar": [10, "apples"] }' ) sample = { "blue" : [ 1 , 2 ], "ocean" : "water" } json_string = json . dumps ( sample ) print ( json_string )

它是干什么用的？ 数据科学、数据可视化、机器学习、任务自动化、Web 开发（服务器端）、游戏开发等。

我在哪里可以学习它？ 使用 Python 自动化枯燥的工作- 一个学习 Python 的绝佳在线资源。 它介绍了 Python，然后向您展示了如何解决一些日常任务。

它用于开发交互式网页或应用程序。 大约 97% 的页面在客户端使用 JS 来控制网页行为。 它通常与 HTML 和 CSS 结合使用。

它还拥有大量库，可帮助您轻松解决各种类型的任务。

它看起来像什么？

 var data = JSON . parse ( ' { "foo": 1, "bar": [10, "apples"] } ' ); var sample = { " blue " : [ 1 , 2 ], " ocean " : " water " }; var json_string = JSON . stringify ( sample ); console . log ( json_string )

它是干什么用的？ 前端 Web 开发、后端 Web 开发（Node.js）、游戏开发（浏览器游戏）、移动应用程序（React Native）

我在哪里可以学习它？ 我们还需要多说吗？

爪哇

它曾经并且仍然是一种非常流行的语言，主要是因为 Java 虚拟机能够在许多不同类型的设备上运行 Java 代码。 它的语法类似于 C 和 C++，但与它们不同的是，它不是低级语言，这使得它对初学者更加友好。

它看起来像什么？

 import com.google.gson.Gson ; public class JsonExample { public static void main ( String [] args ) { Gson gson = new Gson (); String json = "{ \"foo\": 1, \"bar\": [ \"10\", \"apples\"] }" ; MyJsonObject obj = gson . fromJson ( json , MyJsonObject . class ); System . out . println ( obj . getFoo ()); for ( String bar : obj . getBar ()) { System . out . println ( bar ); } obj = new MyJsonObject ( 2 , new String [] { "20" , "oranges" }); json = gson . toJson ( obj ); System . out . println ( json ); } }

它是干什么用的？ 我在哪里可以学习它？ 它类似于 Java，被认为是它的直接竞争对手。 如果不涉及不必要的细节，没有什么可添加的。

它看起来像什么？

 using System ; using System.Collections.Generic ; using System.Web.Script.Serialization ; class Program { static void Main () { var people = new Dictionary < string , object > {{ "1" , "John" }, { "2" , "Susan" }}; var serializer = new JavaScriptSerializer (); var json = serializer . Serialize ( people ); Console . WriteLine ( json ); var deserialized = serializer . Deserialize < Dictionary < string , object >>( json ); Console . WriteLine ( deserialized [ "2" ]); var jsonObject = serializer . DeserializeObject ( @"{ ""foo"": 1, ""bar"": [10, ""apples""] }" ); var data = jsonObject as Dictionary < string , object >; var array = data [ "bar" ] as object []; Console . WriteLine ( array [ 1 ]); } }

它是干什么用的？ 我在哪里可以学习它？ 微软的官方页面- 它有一堆视频教程和文档

去

它能够以非阻塞方式支持并发，当您需要运行多个并发进程时，它可以扩展。 它仍然是一种相对年轻的语言，可能有其缺点。 然而，许多人喜欢使用 Go 的简单性，越来越多的公司开始在他们的一些流程中使用它。

它看起来像什么？

 package main import "encoding/json" import "fmt" func main () { var data interface {} err := json . Unmarshal ([] byte ( `{"foo":1, "bar":[10, "apples"]}` ), & data ) if err == nil { fmt . Println ( data ) } else { fmt . Println ( err ) } sample := map [ string ] interface {}{ "blue" : [] interface {}{ 1 , 2 }, "ocean" : "water" , } json_string , err := json . Marshal ( sample ) if err == nil { fmt . Println ( string ( json_string )) } else { fmt . Println ( err ) } }

它是干什么用的？ 基础设施、Web 开发（服务器端）、数据科学、机器学习

我在哪里可以学习它？ 准备好学习编程了吗？

在这篇简短的文章中，我们为您提供了一些指导，帮助您选择您的第一种编程语言。 决定上面的编程路径之一，然后选择一种语言来配合它。

如果您无法决定，那么我们建议您选择 Python。 这是清单上最容易学习的一种，您将有很好的机会找到一份工作。

如果您对每种语言的流行程度以及使用该语言的开发人员的平均薪水感兴趣，请查看StackOverflow 2022 调查。

你在等什么？ 选择一种语言并开始！ 在评论中让我们知道您选择了哪一个。



原文:

Are you feeling overwhelmed and don't know which programming language you should learn first? Well, you've come to the right place.

In this post, we'll list some of the most popular programming languages and explain what you can do with them. The goal of this post is to help you pick one of the languages and start coding.

Learning programming concepts is much more important than learning any specific programming language. That's because the concepts are independent of programming languages. If you'd like an intro on that, feel free to download our e-book.

Let's start with the list.

Note: All the code examples represent roughly the same program written in different languages for easy comparison.

Python

Python is one of the most popular programming languages in the last decade, especially for beginners.

It has a large number of libraries that can be used for various branches of the industry. It's also extremely popular for QA automation tasks.

It's a very easy language to learn: it uses indentation instead of curly braces and it does not need to use semicolons. Since it's easy to learn, it's often the language of choice for people that are not programmers - like scientists, mechanical engineers, accountants, etc.

What it looks like?

import json
data = json.loads('{ "foo": 1, "bar": [10, "apples"] }') sample = { "blue": [1,2], "ocean": "water" }
json_string = json.dumps(sample) print(json_string)

What's it used for? data science, data visualization, machine learning, task automation, web development (server-side), game development, etc.

Where can I learn it? Automate the boring stuff with Python - a great online resource to learn Python from. It has an introduction to Python after which it shows you how to solve some everyday tasks.

JavaScript

JavaScript or JS is the programming language that some people love and some people hate. It's used to develop interactive web pages or applications. About 97% of pages use JS on the client-side to control web page behavior. It's usually used in combination with HTML and CSS.

It also has a large number of libraries that help you easily solve various types of tasks.

What it looks like?

var data = JSON.parse('{ "foo": 1, "bar": [10, "apples"] }'); var sample = { "blue": [1,2], "ocean": "water" };
var json_string = JSON.stringify(sample); console.log(json_string)

What's it used for? frontend web development, backend web development (Node.js), game development (browser games), mobile applications (React Native)

Where can I learn it? The Odin Project - a page that offers a full-stack JavaScript path for free. Do we need to say more?

Java

(Do not confuse Java with JavaScript, they are not similar at all)

Java is another versatile programming language. It was and still is a pretty popular language, mainly because of the Java virtual machine that enables to run Java code on many different types of devices. Its syntax is similar to C and C++, but unlike them, it's not a low-level language, which makes it much more friendly to beginners.

What it looks like?

import com.google.gson.Gson; public class JsonExample { public static void main(String[] args) { Gson gson = new Gson(); String json = "{ \"foo\": 1, \"bar\": [ \"10\", \"apples\"] }"; MyJsonObject obj = gson.fromJson(json, MyJsonObject.class); System.out.println(obj.getFoo()); for(String bar : obj.getBar()) { System.out.println(bar); } obj = new MyJsonObject(2, new String[] { "20", "oranges" }); json = gson.toJson(obj); System.out.println(json); } }

What's it used for? web development (server-side) - good for large enterprise applications, desktop applications (Windows and Linux), mobile development (Android apps - however, Kotlin is now more popular for Android developers)

Where can I learn it? Programiz - a site that explains Java from scratch

C#

C# is a programming language developed by Microsoft. It's similar to Java and is considered to be its direct rival. There is nothing much to add without going into unnecessary details.

What it looks like?

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Web.Script.Serialization; class Program
{ static void Main() { var people = new Dictionary<string, object> {{"1", "John"}, {"2", "Susan"}}; var serializer = new JavaScriptSerializer(); var json = serializer.Serialize(people); Console.WriteLine(json); var deserialized = serializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, object>>(json); Console.WriteLine(deserialized["2"]); var jsonObject = serializer.DeserializeObject(@"{ ""foo"": 1, ""bar"": [10, ""apples""] }"); var data = jsonObject as Dictionary<string, object>; var array = data["bar"] as object[]; Console.WriteLine(array[1]); }
}

What's it used for? web development (server-side), desktop applications (Windows), game development (Unity), mobile development (Xamarin)

Where can I learn it? Microsoft's official page - it has a bunch of video tutorials and documentation

Go

Go or Golang is a fast and easy-to-learn programming language developed by a team at Google. It has the ability to support concurrency in a non-blocking way which makes it scalable when you need to run multiple concurrent processes. It's still a relatively young language, which can have its disadvantages. However, many people love using Go for its simplicity and more and more companies are starting to use it for some of their processes.

What it looks like?

package main import "encoding/json"
import "fmt" func main() { var data interface{} err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(`{"foo":1, "bar":[10, "apples"]}`), &data) if err == nil { fmt.Println(data) } else { fmt.Println(err) } sample := map[string]interface{}{ "blue": []interface{}{1, 2}, "ocean": "water", } json_string, err := json.Marshal(sample) if err == nil { fmt.Println(string(json_string)) } else { fmt.Println(err) }
}

What's it used for? infrastructure, web development (server-side), data science, machine learning

Where can I learn it? Golang BootCamp - a book that has everything you need to know to start with Golang

Ready to learn programming?

In this short post we gave you some directions which will help you choose your first programming language. Decide on one of the programming paths above and then pick a language to go with it.

If you can't decide, then we'd suggest you pick Python. It's the easiest one to learn on the list and you'll have a good chance finding a job with it.

If you're interested in the popularity of each language and what's the average salary of developers working in that language, then check out the StackOverflow 2022 survey.

What are you waiting for? Pick one language and start! Let us know in the comments which one you've picked.
item.author: qavsdev

item.title: 什么是 Javascript 中的 Nullish Coalescing（或 ??） | What is Nullish Coalescing (or ??) in Javascript
item.description:
在 Javascript 中，无效的合并运算符，或?? 运算符用于在左侧为null或undefined时返回右侧。 为了更好地理解，让我们看几个例子：

 // Is set to 0 let x = 0 ?? " hello " ; // Is set to goodbye let y = undefined ?? " goodbye " ; // Is set to hello let z = null ?? " hello " ; // Is set to false let a = false ?? " goodbye " ;

例如，可以为在某些情况下返回 undefined 的函数提供默认值：

 let myFunction = ( a ) => { if ( a >= 5 ) { return " hello world " ; } } // Will return "goodbye world", since `myFunction(4)` returns undefined. let runFunction = myFunction ( 4 ) ?? " goodbye world " ;

逻辑 OR 运算符之间的差异

过去，我们通常使用逻辑OR ( || ) 运算符在 Javascript 中设置默认值。 它具有相同的功能，如果左边的第一个值不满足某些条件，它会设置一个值。 然而， || 如果左边的值是虚假的，则运算符返回右边的值 - 并且有很多虚假值，如下面的列表所示。

虚假值

  • false
  • 任何空字符串，即""
  • null
  • undefined
  • NaN

因此，使用|| 运算符意味着如果一个函数返回值0 ，而我们确实想要使用0值，我们将无法使用 - 因为0是假的。 使用 nullish 合并运算符 ( ?? )， 0是一个有效值，因为它仅在值为null或undefined时触发：

 // Is set to 0 let x = 0 ?? 5 ; // Is set to 5 let y = 0 || 5 ;

同样，如果字符串为空， || 运算符将默认为右侧 - 这并不总是所需的行为。 运算符让我们避免这种情况：

 // Is set to "" let x = "" ?? " default text " ; // Is set to "default text" let x = "" || " default text " ;

链接无效合并运算符

也可以链接空值合并运算符，如下所示：

 // Is set to "default text" let x = null ?? undefined ?? " default text " ;

但是你不能用逻辑||链接它运算符，除非带括号：

 // Errors out: let x = 0 || undefined ?? " default text " ; // Returns "default text"; let y = ( 0 || undefined ) ?? " default text " ;


原文:

In Javascript, the nullish coalescing operator, or ?? operator is used to return the right hand side whenever the left hand side is null or undefined. To understand a little bit better, let's look at a few examples:

// Is set to 0
let x = 0 ?? "hello"; // Is set to goodbye
let y = undefined ?? "goodbye"; // Is set to hello
let z = null ?? "hello"; // Is set to false
let a = false ?? "goodbye";

The nullish coalescing operator is useful in situations where something can be returned as either null or undefined, and helps us tighten up our code. For example, a function that returns undefined in some situations can be provided with a default value:

let myFunction = (a) => { if(a >= 5) { return "hello world"; }
} // Will return "goodbye world", since `myFunction(4)` returns undefined.
let runFunction = myFunction(4) ?? "goodbye world";

Differences between the logical OR operator

In the past, we typically set default values in Javascript using the logical OR (||) operator. It has the same kind of functionality, in that it sets a value if the first value on the left doesn't meet certain criteria. However, the || operator returns the right hand value if the left hand value is anything falsy - and there are a lot of falsy values, as shown in the list below.

Falsy Values

  • false
  • 0 or -0 or 0n
  • any empty string, i.e. ""
  • null
  • undefined
  • NaN

As such, using the || operator would mean that if a function returned the value 0, and we really did want to use the 0 value, we wouldn't be able to - since 0 is falsy. With the nullish coalescing operator (??), 0 is a valid value since it only triggers if a value is null or undefined:

// Is set to 0
let x = 0 ?? 5; // Is set to 5
let y = 0 || 5;

Similarly, if a string is empty, the || operator would default to the right hand side - which is not always the desired behaviour. The ?? operator lets us avoid that:

// Is set to ""
let x = "" ?? "default text"; // Is set to "default text"
let x = "" || "default text";

Chaining the nullish coalescing operator

It is also possible to chain the nullish coalescing operator, as shown below:

// Is set to "default text"
let x = null ?? undefined ?? "default text";

But you cannot chain it with the logical || operator, unless with parenthesis:

// Errors out:
let x = 0 || undefined ?? "default text"; // Returns "default text";
let y = (0 || undefined) ?? "default text";
item.author: smpnjn

item.title: 在 Lua 中配置 Neovim | Configure Neovim in Lua
item.description:
我已经有一段时间没有写一篇 Vim 文章了。 最后，在为 Lua 配置了 Neovim 设置后，我有了一些想法。 几个月前我最近迁移到了 Ubuntu，这对 Windows 7 来说是一个很酷的变化！

在本文中，我们将了解如何为 Lua 设置 neovim。 从 Neovim 0.5 开始，它支持开箱即用的 lua，因此在最近的 0.7 版本中，它添加了更多对 lua 的原生支持，使得配置和使用 neovim 变得更加容易。 因此，我们将看到如何使用 lua 将所有 200 行 vimscript 转换为 lua（我们甚至可以拥有包和模块：）我们将介绍如何配置您的键盘映射、提取所有插件、vim 选项和其他自定义.

为什么要迁移到 Lua？

我已经使用 Vimscript 很长一段时间了，根据我的需要对其进行配置，还制作了一些插件，如frontmatter 、 dj.vim和commenter ，它们在使用和可定制性方面非常笨重且不健壮。 我最近想完全使用 Neovim，我有点卡在 Vimscript 中，我的一些插件对我有用，但可能对其他人不起作用，它可能是一个乱码 vimscript 转储。 所以，如果可以的话，为什么不拥有完整的 Neovim 本地经验。 它现在已经内置了对 LSP、调试、自动命令等的支持。 如果你有 Neovim 0.5+，你应该很好地去完整的 lua。

备份 Neovim 配置

首先，保持原始 neovim/vim 初始化文件的安全，备份它们，制作副本并使用不同的名称保存，例如nvim_config.vim 。 如果您已经将所有配置文件备份为 ansible 脚本或 dotfiles GitHub 存储库，则可以继续。

但是不要将init.vim文件保留在~/.config/nvim文件夹中，它可能会在我们配置 lua 脚本后覆盖。

基本配置

所以，我假设你已经设置了 Neovim，如果没有，你需要遵循一些简单的步骤，比如下载包并确保你的 neovim 环境首先使用 vimscript。 设置好 neovim 并进行基本配置后，您现在可以开始迁移到 lua。
在与您的init.lua文件相同的路径中创建一个init.lua文件，即在~/.config/nvim或~/AppData/Local/nvim/对于 Windows。 这就是为什么建议将初始配置 vimscript 文件保存在安全的地方。 在从 vimscript 迁移到 lua 时，一旦创建了 lua 文件并且下次重新启动 neovim 时，默认设置将来自init.lua而不是init.vim ，因此请做好准备。

首先，您需要配置一些选项，例如number 、 syntax highlighting 、 tabs ，当然还有一些keymaps 。 我们可以使用vim.opt方法在 vim 中使用 lua 语法设置选项。 因此，某些相应的 vim 选项将转换如下：

如果您的 vimrc 或 init.vim 中有以下类型的设置：

 -- vimscript set number set tabstop = 4 set shiftwidth = 4 set softtabstop = 0 set expandtab set noswapfile

以上设置迁移到lua语法如下：

 --lua vim . opt . number = true vim . opt . tabstop = 4 vim . opt . shiftwidth = 4 vim . opt . softtabstop = 0 vim . opt . expandtab = true vim . opt . swapfile = false

您可以相应地在配置文件中设置其他选项。 如果你厌倦了编写vim.opt. 一次又一次，您可以使用设置为vim.opt的变量，然后访问该变量以设置选项。 如下几行：

 local set = vim . opt set . number = true set . tabstop = 4 set . shiftwidth = 4 set . softtabstop = 0 set . expandtab = true set . swapfile = false

我们可以在 lua 中创建一个变量，例如local variable_name = something所以，我们创建了一个变量set ，该变量集分配给vim.opt的值，这是 lua 中的一个方法（函数），用于从 vimscript 环境中设置选项。 最后，访问set关键字来设置选项。 不需要使用set word，你可以使用任何你想要的。

设置基本选项后，您可以在命令模式下使用:so %获取文件。 就像您通常获取配置文件一样。

在命令模式下使用 Lua

我们可以使用 lua 命令在 neovim 的命令模式下使用 lua 函数或任何其他命令。 只需在命令前加上:lua ，然后，您就可以使用 lua 语法，如函数调用或设置变量、记录事物等。

添加键盘映射

现在，我们有了一些基本的配置设置，我们可以快速获取键盘映射。 在 lua 中设置键映射并不难。 要创建键盘映射，我们有两种选择。 一种与 Neovim 兼容，另一种与 Vim 兼容。

  1. 就个人而言，我没有看到使用方面的区别，但vim.keymap.set是nvim_set_keymap的包装器。 因此，这实际上是您想要使用的个人喜好问题。

    因此，这两个函数都有非常相似的参数：

     vim . keymap . set ({ mode }, { lhs }, { rhs }, { options }) vim . api . nvim_set_keymap ({ mode }, { lhs }, { rhs }, { options })

    让我们看一个基本的映射示例：

     vim.keymap.set('n', 'Y', 'yy', {noremap = false})

    在这里，我们在n普通模式下将Shift+y与键yy映射。 第一个参数是模式，可以是单模式，如'n' ， 'v' ， 'i'等，也可以是多模式表，如{'n', 'v'} , {'v', 'i'}等。

    下一个参数也是一个字符串，但它应该是触发映射的关键。 在这种情况下，我们使用了Y ，即Shift + y ，它可以是您想要映射的任何快捷键。

    第三个参数是字符串，它将是使用密钥后要执行的命令。 这里我们使用了键yy ，如果地图来自命令模式，您将使用类似':commands_to_be executed'作为第三个参数。

    第四个可选参数可以包含特殊参数。 我们设置了一个默认选项noremap为 true，选项不是字符串而是 lua 表，所以它可以类似于 python 字典或具有键值对的结构的映射类型。

    键映射中更重要的一个方面可能与领导键有关，您可以通过使用vim.g的全局 vim 选项来设置您的领导键，并访问mapleader将其设置为您想要的键。 这将使leader密钥对我们可用，此后我们可以将领导者密钥映射到自定义映射中。

    在这里，我将我的领导键设置为<Space>键。 现在，我们可以将键映射到 vimscript 中现有的键映射。 让我们先映射一些基本的键盘映射，然后在设置插件之后，我们可以进入插件特定的映射。

    您也可以使用具有相同参数的vim.api.nvim_set_keymap函数。

     vim . keymap . set ( 'n' , '<leader>w' , ':w<CR>' ,{ noremap = true }) vim . keymap . set ( 'n' , '<leader>q' , ':q!<CR>' ,{ noremap = true }) vim . keymap . set ( 'n' , '<leader>s' , ':so %<CR>' ,{ noremap = true }) vim . keymap . set ( 'n' , '<leader>ev' , ':vsplit $MYVIMRC<CR>' ,{ noremap = true }) vim . keymap . set ( 'n' , '<leader>sv' , ':w<CR>:so %<CR>:q<CR>' ,{ noremap = true }) OR vim . api . nvim_set_keymap ( 'n' , '<leader>w' , ':w<CR>' ,{ noremap = true }) vim . api . nvim_set_keymap ( 'n' , '<leader>q' , ':q!<CR>' ,{ noremap = true }) vim . api . nvim_set_keymap ( 'n' , '<leader>s' , ':so %<CR>' ,{ noremap = true }) vim . api . nvim_set_keymap ( 'n' , '<leader>ev' , ':vsplit $MYVIMRC<CR>' ,{ noremap = true }) vim . api . nvim_set_keymap ( 'n' , '<leader>sv' , ':w<CR>:so %<CR>:q<CR>' ,{ noremap = true })

    如果你不喜欢一次又一次地编写vim.keymap.set或vim.api.nvim_set_keymap ，你可以为它创建一个更简单的函数。 在 lua 中，通过指定函数的范围（即 local 后跟function关键字，最后是函数名和括号）来创建函数就像变量一样。 函数体由end关键字终止。

     function map ( mode , lhs , rhs , opts ) local options = { noremap = true } if opts then options = vim . tbl_extend ( "force" , options , opts ) end vim . api . nvim_set_keymap ( mode , lhs , rhs , options ) end

    现在，在这个函数map中，我们传递了与vim.keymap.set函数所采用的相同的参数，但我们只是通过默认添加noremap = true以更短且更安全的方式解析函数。 所以这只是调用 vim.keymap.set 函数的辅助函数或详细函数。

    要使用这个函数，我们可以简单地调用map并使用与先前函数相同的参数。

     map ( 'n' , '<leader>w' , ':w<CR>' ) map ( 'n' , '<leader>q' , ':q!<CR>' ) map ( 'n' , '<leader>s' , ':so %<CR>' )

    但是请注意，我们没有传递{noremap = true}因为它会默认通过自定义映射函数传递给vim.api.nvim_set_keymap或vim.keymap.set函数。

    如果您想要更多示例，这里有一些特定于语言的附加映射，用于使用 neovim 实例编译或运行脚本。

     -- vimscript nnoremap cpp : ! c ++ % - o % : r && % : r < CR > nnoremap c , : ! gcc % - o % : r && % : r < CR > nnoremap py : ! python %< cr > nnoremap go : ! go run %< cr > nnoremap sh : ! bash %< CR > -- lua map ( 'n' , 'cpp' ':!c++ % -o %:r && %:r<CR>' ) map ( 'n' , 'c,' ':!gcc % -o %:r && %:r<CR>' ) map ( 'n' , 'py' ':!python %<cr>' ) map ( 'n' , 'go' ':!go run %<cr>' ) map ( 'n' , 'sh' ':!bash %<cr>' )

    所以，这就是我们如何在 lua 中设置我们的键盘映射。 您可以根据需要自定义此功能。 这些只是为了了解如何减少设置中的重复内容。

    如果你真的被卡住并且不想将这些映射转换为 lua，那么我有一个可以为你做的功能，请查看我的 dotfile repo -> keymapper

    添加插件管理器

    现在，我们真的缺少一些插件，不是吗？ 所以，neovim 社区有一些不错的选择来使用用核心 lua 编写的新插件管理器。 完全进入 lua 通常是一个好主意，而不是在 vimscript 和 lua 之间来回切换。

    因此， Packer是 Lua 中 Neovim 的新插件管理器，还有其他插件管理器，例如paq 。 如果您不想使用插件管理器进行切换，您仍然可以使用基于 vim 的插件管理器，例如Vim-Plug 。

    所以，让我们将 Packer 插件管理器安装到 Neovim 中。 我们只需在控制台中运行以下命令并确保插件管理器配置正确。

     # Linux git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/wbthomason/packer.nvim\ ~/.local/share/nvim/site/pack/packer/start/packer.nvim # Windows git clone https://github.com/wbthomason/packer.nvim "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\nvim-data\site\pack\packer\start\packer.nvim"

    现在，如果您打开一个新的 neovim 实例并尝试运行命令:PackerClean ，并且没有弹出错误，则表明您已正确配置它。 您现在可以继续安装插件。 是的！ 插件时间！

     return require ( 'packer' ). startup ( function () end )

    首先尝试获取文件，如果它抛出错误，则不应尝试修复 Packer 的安装路径。 如果命令成功，我们终于可以拉出一些插件。

    以下是一些您可以使用的插件，无论您有什么语言偏好。 这包括状态行的基本开发图标以及资源管理器窗口文件图标。 像往常一样，添加你的插件并让它们成为你的。

     return require ( 'packer' ). startup ( function () use 'wbthomason/packer.nvim' use 'tpope/vim-fugitive' use { 'nvim-lualine/lualine.nvim' , requires = { 'kyazdani42/nvim-web-devicons' , opt = true } } use 'tiagofumo/vim-nerdtree-syntax-highlight' use 'kyazdani42/nvim-web-devicons' use 'vim-airline/vim-airline' use 'vim-airline/vim-airline-themes' end )

    添加插件列表后，您需要获取文件，然后使用命令:PackerInstall安装插件。 此功能将在获取文件后安装所有插件，因此请确保您不要忘记它。

    颜色和颜色主题

    当然，您可能会喜欢为 neovim 设置一些美观且美观的设置！ 在 Neovim 中，我们已经有各种各样的配置可供设置，例如配色方案、GUI 颜色、终端颜色等。您可以从GitHub的大量出色配色方案中选择配色方案。

    选择主题后，将其插入我们刚刚创建的打包程序插件列表并获取文件，最后运行:PackerInstall 。 这应该安装插件。 然后，您可以根据自己的喜好设置颜色方案，首先使用命令:colorscheme colorscheme_name在当前实例上临时查看颜色方案。

     return require ( 'packer' ). startup ( function () use 'wbthomason/packer.nvim' -- use 'Mofiqul/dracula.nvim' -- end )

    然后，您可以添加命令以将其设置为 Neovim 的默认配色方案。

     vim . cmd [[silent! colorscheme dracula]]

    您可以使用 augroup 和设置颜色方案命令分别更改背景颜色、文本颜色、图标样式以及终端和 gui 样式。

     vim . api . nvim_command ( [[ augroup ChangeBackgroudColour autocmd colorscheme * :hi normal termbg=#000030 termfg=#ffffff autocmd colorscheme * :hi Directory ctermfg=#ffffff augroup END ]] ) vim . o . termguicolors = true

    在这里，我使用了 Neovim 终端变体的背景色和前景色。 同样对于目录资源管理器，即 netrw，我已经更改了终端前景。 您可以根据需要进行配置，尽管这仍然是 vimscripty，但 Neovim 中很快就会提供自动命令和自动组。

    分离配置

    如果您希望将所有配置保存在一个文件中，即init.lua文件，则可以，但您可以将诸如keymaps 、 plugins 、 custom_options类的东西分开，或者如果您将lsp配置放入单独的 lua 包或创建单独的模块。 这有助于根据要求加载内容，并且看起来也易于阅读，这使得在不破坏整个配置的情况下测试内容变得更加容易。

    当然，这两种方法都会有个人喜好和优缺点，你可以选择适合你风格的任何东西。

    创建单独的包

    要创建一个单独的包，我们可以简单地在与init.vim相同的文件夹中添加一个文件，即在文件夹~/.config/nvim或 Windows 的等效文件夹中。 包名可以是任何带有lua扩展名的有效文件名。

    例如，您可以为所有键盘映射创建一个包，并按照您想要加载它们的顺序将其加载到init.lua中。 它可以在顶部，或者如果您在 init 文件中有某些较低的基本设置，这些可能不会反映或加载到键盘映射包中，因此最好在设置了一些核心设置后加载它们。

    让我们创建包并将我们所有的地图转储到键映射文件包中。

     -- ~/.config/nvim/keymap.lua function map ( mode , lhs , rhs , opts ) local options = { noremap = true } if opts then options = vim . tbl_extend ( "force" , options , opts ) end vim . api . nvim_set_keymap ( mode , lhs , rhs , options ) end map ( 'n' , '<leader>w' , ':w<CR>' ) map ( 'n' , '<leader>q' , ':q!<CR>' ) map ( 'n' , '<leader>s' , ':so %<CR>' ) map ( 'n' , 'cpp' ':!c++ % -o %:r && %:r<CR>' ) map ( 'n' , 'c,' ':!gcc % -o %:r && %:r<CR>' ) map ( 'n' , 'py' ':!python %<cr>' ) map ( 'n' , 'go' ':!go run %<cr>' ) map ( 'n' , 'sh' ':!bash %<cr>' ) -- more keymaps

    因此，如果您没有任何与插件相关的键盘映射，这可能会起作用，因为它需要那些可用的函数或对象才能执行。 因此，我们可能还想分离插件并首先将它们加载到我们的键盘映射或 init 文件中。

    现在，需要一种获取包裹的方法。 是的，基本上就像 python 或任何其他编程语言中的 import 一样，lua 具有用于导入包的require关键字。 由于init文件和keymaps在同一个文件夹路径中，我们可以简单地说，在我们的init.lua文件中require("keymap") 。 现在，这取决于您要加载此包的配置。 在顶部，即在 neovim 实例的开头或加载插件之后。

     -- init.lua require ( "keymaps" ) -- At the top -- OR -- After loading Packer plugins

    因此，现在您可以根据需要分离所有配置。 这就像拆分一个谜题并再次将它们组合在一起。 可以为plugins 、 options或lsp配置创建类似的包。

    创建一个单独的模块

    现在，我们已经了解了如何创建一个 lua 包并在 neovim 中加载。 我们也可以在 neovim 配置中创建模块。 例如，首先加载 init 文件，其他文件可能不需要，因此默认情况下不加载，仅在require时加载。 因此，我们可以在 lua 中创建一个带有文件夹的模块，在其中，我们可以像之前的方法一样拥有包。 此外，当我们需要该模块时，每个模块都可以首先加载一个 init 文件。 因此，模块中的其余包随后可供使用。

    • 仅在需要时加载（字面意思是需要）
    • 每个模块都可以在需要时首先加载一个init.lua文件。
    • 从外部需要模块中的包 -> require('module_name.package_name')

    因此，在 neovim 中，我们需要创建一个lua文件夹来放置我们所有的模块，以便正确选择 lua 运行时。 在这个 lua 文件夹中，我们可以创建一个模块，基本上是一个文件夹。 这个文件夹的名字可以是任何你喜欢的名字，我喜欢保留我的昵称，你可以使用任何你喜欢的名字。

     # ~/.config/nvim -- init.lua -- lua/ -- module_name/ -- init.lua -- package_name.lua -- keymaps.lua

    现在，我们可以在这个模块中创建包。 您可以在此文件夹中移动您的键盘映射包。 您基本上可以将所有键盘映射转储到一个 lua 文件中，然后从 init 文件中导入它。 同样，您可以在模块内创建一个包，然后从模块的初始化文件（本地初始化文件~/.config/nvim/lua/module_name/init.lua ）或全局初始化文件（ ~/.config/nvim/init.lua ）。

    要加载包，您必须使用相同的 require 语句，无论您从哪里导入，即从模块或全局 init 文件导入。 现在，我们可以使用 keymaps 包从模块 init 文件中导入，然后从全局 init 文件中导入模块。 要导入模块，我们可以简单地将 require 字符串中的模块名称用作require("module_name") 。

     -- ~/.config/nvim -- lua/module_name/options.lua vim . opt . number = true vim . opt . tabstop = 4 vim . opt . swapfile = false -- lua/module_name/plugins.lua require ( "module_name.options" ) return require ( 'packer' ). startup ( function () use 'wbthomason/packer.nvim' --plugins end ) -- lua/module_name/keymap.lua require ( "module_name.plugins" ) -- maps() -- lua/module_name/init.lua require ( "module_name.keymaps) -- init.lua require(" module_name ")

    因此，这就是我们如何使用 lua 在 neovim 中为配置创建模块和包的方式。 这也是一种用lua创建自己的neovim插件的结构！

    如需进一步参考，您可以查看我的dotfiles 。

    参考

    • 为 Lua 配置 Neovim
    • 结论

      所以，这只是关于如何为 lua 配置 neovim 的基本概述。 创造这样一个个性化的环境并在其中玩耍并获得乐趣是一种很棒的体验。 您可能希望通过这个小指南为 Lua 配置 Neovim。 也许在下一篇文章中，我会在 Neovim 中设置 LSP。 如果您有任何疑问或反馈，请告诉我。 感谢您的阅读。

      快乐维明 :)



原文:

It has been a while since I have written a Vim article. Finally, I got some ideas after configuring my Neovim setup for Lua. I recently migrated to Ubuntu a couple of months back and it has been a cool change from Windows 7!

In this article, we'll see how you can set up neovim for Lua. Since Neovim 0.5, it supports lua out of the box, so in the recent release 0.7, it added more native support to lua making it a lot easier to configure and play with neovim. So, we will see how we can use lua to convert all the 200 liner vimscript into lua (We can even have packages and modules:) We will cover how to configure your keymaps, pull up all the plugins, vim options, and other customizations.

Why move to Lua?

I have used Vimscript for quite a while now, configured it as per my needs, and also made a few plugins like frontmatter, dj.vim, and commenter which are quite clunky and not robust in terms of usage and customizability. Vimscript is good but it's a bit messy when you want extreme customization.

I recently wanted to go full Neovim, I was kind of stuck in Vimscript and some of my plugins work for me but it might not work for others, it might be a piece of gibberish vimscript dumped. So, Why not have full native experience in Neovim if you can. It has now baked-in support for LSP, Debugging, Autocommands, and so much more. If you have Neovim 0.5+ you should be good to go full lua.

Backup Neovim Config

Firstly, keep your original neovim/vim init files safe, back them up, make a copy and save it with a different name like nvim_config.vim. If you already have all your config files backed up as an ansible script or dotfiles GitHub repository, you can proceed ahead.

But don't keep the init.vim file as it is in the ~/.config/nvim folder, it might override after we configure the lua scripts.

Basic Configuration

So, I assume you have set up Neovim, If not you need to follow some simple steps like downloading the package and making sure your neovim environment is working with vimscript first. The Neovim Wiki provides great documentation on how to install neovim on various platforms using different methods.

After your neovim is set up and you have a basic configuration, you can now start to migrate into lua.
Create a init.lua file in the same path as your init.lua file is i.e. at ~/.config/nvim or ~/AppData/Local/nvim/ for Windows. That's why it is recommended to keep the initial configuration vimscript file in a safe place. While migrating from vimscript to lua, once the lua file is created and the next time you restart neovim, the default settings will be from init.lua and not init.vim, so be prepared.

Firstly, you need to configure some options like number, syntax highlighting, tabs, and some keymaps of course. We can use the vim.opt method to set options in vim using lua syntax. So, certain corresponding vim options would be converted as follows:

If you have the following kind of settings in your vimrc or init.vim:

-- vimscript
set number
set tabstop=4 set shiftwidth=4 set softtabstop=0 set expandtab set noswapfile

The above settings are migrated into lua syntax as follows:

--lua
vim.opt.number = true
vim.opt.tabstop = 4
vim.opt.shiftwidth = 4
vim.opt.softtabstop = 0
vim.opt.expandtab = true
vim.opt.swapfile = false

You can set other options in your config file accordingly. If you get sick of writing vim.opt. again and again, you can use a variable set to vim.opt and then access that variable to set the options. Something of the lines as below:

local set = vim.opt set.number = true
set.tabstop = 4
set.shiftwidth = 4
set.softtabstop = 0
set.expandtab = true
set.swapfile = false

We can create a variable in lua like local variable_name = something so, we have created a variable set which is assigned to the value of vim.opt which is a method(function) in lua to set the options from the vimscript environment. Finally, access the set keyword to set the options. Using the set word is not necessary, you can use whatever you want.

After setting up the basic options, you can source the file with :so % from the command mode. Just normally as you source the config files.

Using Lua in Command Mode

We can use the lua functions or any other commands from the command mode in neovim using the lua command. Just prefix the command with :lua and after that, you can use lua syntax like function calling or setting variables, logging things, etc.

Adding Keymaps

Now, that we have some basic config setup, we can quickly get the keymaps. It's not that hard to make keymaps to set up in lua. To create keymaps, we have two options. One is compatible with Neovim and the other is compatible with Vim as well.

  1. vim.keymap.set OR
  2. vim.api.nvim_set_keymap

Personally, I don't see a difference in terms of usage but vim.keymap.set is a wrapper around nvim_set_keymap. So, it is really a matter of personal preference which you want to use.

So, both the functions have quite similar parameters:

vim.keymap.set({mode}, {lhs}, {rhs}, {options}) vim.api.nvim_set_keymap({mode}, {lhs}, {rhs}, {options})

The advantage of vim.keymap.set over vim.api.nvim_set_keymap is that it allows directly calling lua functions rather than calling vimscripty way like :lua function(), so we directly can use lua code in the RHS part of the function parameter.

Let's take a basic example mapping:

vim.keymap.set('n', 'Y', 'yy', {noremap = false})

Here, we have mapped Shift+y with the keys yy in normal mode. The first argument is the mode, it can be a single-mode like 'n', 'v', 'i', etc., or a multi-mode table like {'n', 'v'}, {'v', 'i'}, etc.

The next parameter is also a string but it should be the key for triggering the mapping. In this case, we have used Y which is Shift + y, it can be any key shortcut you want to map.

The third parameter is the string which will be the command to be executed after the key has been used. Here we have used the keys yy, if the map is from a command mode, you will be using something like ':commands_to_be executed' as the third parameter.

The fourth parameter which is optional can contain special arguments. We have set a default option which is noremap as true, the options are not string but lua tables instead, so it can be similar to python dictionary or a map kind of a structure with a key value pair.

One more important aspect in keymapping might about the leader key, you can set your leader key by using the global vim options with vim.g and access mapleader to set it to the key you wish. This will make the leader key available to us and thereafter we can map the leader key in custom mappings.

Here, I have set my leader key to the <Space> key. Now, we can map keys to the existing keymaps in the vimscript. Let's map some basic keymaps first and then after setting up the plugins,we can move into plugin-specific mappings.

You can also use vim.api.nvim_set_keymap function with the same parameters as well.

vim.keymap.set('n', '<leader>w', ':w<CR>',{noremap = true})
vim.keymap.set('n', '<leader>q', ':q!<CR>',{noremap = true})
vim.keymap.set('n', '<leader>s', ':so %<CR>',{noremap = true})
vim.keymap.set('n', '<leader>ev', ':vsplit $MYVIMRC<CR>',{noremap = true})
vim.keymap.set('n', '<leader>sv', ':w<CR>:so %<CR>:q<CR>',{noremap = true}) OR vim.api.nvim_set_keymap('n', '<leader>w', ':w<CR>',{noremap = true})
vim.api.nvim_set_keymap('n', '<leader>q', ':q!<CR>',{noremap = true})
vim.api.nvim_set_keymap('n', '<leader>s', ':so %<CR>',{noremap = true})
vim.api.nvim_set_keymap('n', '<leader>ev', ':vsplit $MYVIMRC<CR>',{noremap = true})
vim.api.nvim_set_keymap('n', '<leader>sv', ':w<CR>:so %<CR>:q<CR>',{noremap = true})

If, you don't like writing vim.keymap.set or vim.api.nvim_set_keymap again and again, you can create a simpler function for it. In lua a function is created just like a variable by specifying the scope of the function i.e. local followed by the function keyword and finally the name of the function and parenthesis. The function body is terminated by the end keyword.

function map(mode, lhs, rhs, opts) local options = { noremap = true } if opts then options = vim.tbl_extend("force", options, opts) end vim.api.nvim_set_keymap(mode, lhs, rhs, options)
end

Now, in this function map, we have passed in the same parameters like the vim.keymap.set function takes but we have just parsed the function in a shorter and safer way by adding noremap = true by default. So this is just a helper function or a verbose function for calling the vim.keymap.set function.

To use this function, we can simply call map with the same arguments as given to the prior functions.

map('n', '<leader>w', ':w<CR>')
map('n', '<leader>q', ':q!<CR>')
map('n', '<leader>s', ':so %<CR>')

Notice here, though, we have not passed the {noremap = true} as it will be passed by default to the vim.api.nvim_set_keymap or vim.keymap.set function via the custom map function.

If you want some more examples, here are some additional mapping specific to languages, meant for compiling or running scripts with neovim instance.

-- vimscript nnoremap cpp :!c++ % -o %:r && %:r<CR>
nnoremap c, :!gcc % -o %:r && %:r<CR>
nnoremap py :!python %<cr>
nnoremap go :!go run %<cr>
nnoremap sh :!bash %<CR> -- lua map('n', 'cpp' ':!c++ % -o %:r && %:r<CR>')
map('n', 'c,' ':!gcc % -o %:r && %:r<CR>')
map('n', 'py' ':!python %<cr>')
map('n', 'go' ':!go run %<cr>')
map('n', 'sh' ':!bash %<cr>')

So, this is how we can set up our keymaps in lua. You can customize this function as per your needs. These are just made for the understanding purpose of how to reduce the repetitive stuff in the setup.

If you are really stuck up and not feeling to convert those mappings into lua then I have a function that can do it for you, check out my dotfile repo -> keymapper

Adding Plugin Manager

Now, we really missing some plugins, aren't we? So, the neovim community has some good choices for using a new plugin manager written in core lua. It is usually a good idea to move into lua completely rather than switching to and fro between vimscript and lua.

So, Packer is the new plugin manager for Neovim in Lua, there is other plugin managers out there as well like paq. If you don't want to switch with the plugin manager, you can still use vim-based plugin managers like Vim-Plug.

So, let's install the Packer plugin manager into Neovim. We simply have to run the following command in the console and make sure the plugin manager is configured correctly.

# Linux

git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/wbthomason/packer.nvim\
~/.local/share/nvim/site/pack/packer/start/packer.nvim


# Windows

git clone https://github.com/wbthomason/packer.nvim "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\nvim-data\site\pack\packer\start\packer.nvim"

Now, if you open a new neovim instance and try to run the command :PackerClean, and no error pops out that means you have configured it correctly. You can move ahead to installing plugins now. Yeah! PLUG-IN time!

return require('packer').startup(function()
end)

First try to source the file, if it throws out errors it shouldn't try to fix the installation path of Packer. If the command succeded we can finally pull up some plugins.

Below are some of the plugins that you can use irrespective of what language preferences you would have. This includes basic dev-icons for the status line as well as the explorer window file icons. As usual, add your plugins and make them yours.


return require('packer').startup(function() use 'wbthomason/packer.nvim' use 'tpope/vim-fugitive' use { 'nvim-lualine/lualine.nvim', requires = { 'kyazdani42/nvim-web-devicons', opt = true } } use 'tiagofumo/vim-nerdtree-syntax-highlight' use 'kyazdani42/nvim-web-devicons' use 'vim-airline/vim-airline' use 'vim-airline/vim-airline-themes'
end)

After adding the list of your plugins, you need to source the file and then install the plugins with the command :PackerInstall. This function will install all the plugins after the file has been sourced so make sure you don't forget it.

Colors and Color Themes

You might fancy some good-looking and aesthetic setup for neovim of course! In Neovim, we already have a wide variety of configurations to set up like color schemes, GUI colors, terminal colors, etc. You can pick up a color scheme from a large list of awesome color schemes from GitHub.

After choosing the theme, plug it in the packer plugin list which we just created and source the file and finally run :PackerInstall. This should install the plugin. You can then set the colorscheme as per your preference, first view the color scheme temporarily on the current instance with the command :colorscheme colorscheme_name.

return require('packer').startup(function() use 'wbthomason/packer.nvim' --  use 'Mofiqul/dracula.nvim' --
end)

You can then add the command to set it as the default color scheme for Neovim.

vim.cmd [[silent! colorscheme dracula]]

You can change the background color, text color, icons style and terminal and gui style separately with the augroup and setting the colorscheme commands.

vim.api.nvim_command([[ augroup ChangeBackgroudColour autocmd colorscheme * :hi normal termbg=#000030 termfg=#ffffff autocmd colorscheme * :hi Directory ctermfg=#ffffff augroup END
]])
vim.o.termguicolors = true

Here, I have used the background and foreground colors of the terminal variant of Neovim. Also for the Directory Explorer i.e. netrw, I have changed the terminal foreground. This you can configure as per your needs, Though this is still vimscripty, there are Autocommands and autogroups available sooner in Neovim.

Separating Configurations

If you wish to keep all your config in one file i.e. init.lua file, you can, though you can separate out things like keymaps, plugins, custom_options or if you have lsp configurations into separate lua packages or creating a separate module. This helps in loading up things as per requirement and also looks readable, making it a lot easier to test out things without breaking the whole config.

Definitely, there will be personal preferences and pros and cons of both approaches, you can pick up whatever suits your style.

Creating separate packages

To create a separate package, we can simply add a file in the same folder as init.vim i.e. in the folder ~/.config/nvim or equivalent for windows. The package name can be any valid filename with the lua extension.

For instance, you can create a package for all your keymaps and load it in the init.lua as per the order you want to load them. It can be at the top, or else if you have certain base settings lower in the init file, these might not reflect or load up in the keymap package so better to load them after some of the core settings have been set.

Let's create the package and dump all our maps into the keymap file package.

-- ~/.config/nvim/keymap.lua function map(mode, lhs, rhs, opts) local options = { noremap = true } if opts then options = vim.tbl_extend("force", options, opts) end vim.api.nvim_set_keymap(mode, lhs, rhs, options)
end map('n', '<leader>w', ':w<CR>')
map('n', '<leader>q', ':q!<CR>')
map('n', '<leader>s', ':so %<CR>')
map('n', 'cpp' ':!c++ % -o %:r && %:r<CR>')
map('n', 'c,' ':!gcc % -o %:r && %:r<CR>')
map('n', 'py' ':!python %<cr>')
map('n', 'go' ':!go run %<cr>')
map('n', 'sh' ':!bash %<cr>') -- more keymaps

So, this might work if you don't have any plugin-related keymaps as it would require those functions or objects available to execute. So, we might also want to separate plugins and load them first in our keymaps or in the init file.

Now, there needs to be a way for grabbing a package. Yes, there is basically like import in python or any other programming language, lua has require keyword for importing packages. Since the init file and the keymaps are in the same folder path, we can simply say, require("keymap") in our init.lua file. Now, it depends on your config where you want to load this package. At the top i.e. at the beginning of neovim instance or after loading the plugins down.

-- init.lua require("keymaps") -- At the top
-- OR
-- After loading Packer plugins

So, now you can separate all your configs as per your requirement. It is like splitting up a puzzle and again combining them. Similar package can be created for plugins, options or lsp configurations.

Creating a separate module

Now, we have seen how to create a lua package and loading in neovim. We also can create modules in neovim configuration. For instance, first, the init file is loaded, Other files might not be required hence it is not loaded by default, it is only loaded when requireed. So, we can create a module in lua with a folder, and inside of it, we can have packages as we had in the previous method. Also, every module can have a init file loaded first when we require that module. The rest of the packages in the module are thus made available thereafter.

  • Module is a component not loaded by default
  • Only loaded when required (literally require)
  • Every module can have a init.lua file loaded first when required.
  • Require a package in module from outside -> require('module_name.package_name')

So, in neovim, we need to create a lua folder for placing all our modules so that the lua runtime is picked up correctly. Inside this lua folder, we can create a module basically a folder. This folder name can be anything you like, I like to keep it my nickname, you can use whatever you prefer.

# ~/.config/nvim

-- init.lua
-- lua/
    -- module_name/
        -- init.lua
        -- package_name.lua
        -- keymaps.lua

Now, we can create packages in this module. You can move your keymaps package inside this folder. The keymaps package is nothing here but an example provided in the previous section for creating a package. You can basically dump all your keymaps in a single lua file and then import it from the init file. Similarly you can create a package inside a module and import it from the init file of the module(local init file ~/.config/nvim/lua/module_name/init.lua) or the global init file(~/.config/nvim/init.lua).

To load the packages, you have to use the same require statement irrespective of where you are importing from i.e. either from the module or from the global init file. The require statement would look like the following require("module_name/package_name"). Now, we can use the keymaps package to import from the module init file and then import the module from the global init file. To import a module, we can simply use the module name in the require string as require("module_name").

-- ~/.config/nvim -- lua/module_name/options.lua vim.opt.number = true
vim.opt.tabstop = 4
vim.opt.swapfile = false -- lua/module_name/plugins.lua require("module_name.options")
return require('packer').startup(function() use 'wbthomason/packer.nvim' --plugins
end) -- lua/module_name/keymap.lua require("module_name.plugins")
-- maps() -- lua/module_name/init.lua require("module_name.keymaps) -- init.lua require("module_name")

So, this is how we can create modules and packages for configurations in neovim using lua. This is also a kind of a structure for creating your own neovim plugin with lua!

For further references, you can check out my dotfiles.

References

  • Configure Neovim for Lua
  • Neovim with Lua for beginners
  • TJ Devries Youtube

Conclusion

So, that is just a basic overview of how you can get your neovim configured for lua. It is a great experience to just create such a personalized environment and play with it and have fun. You might have hopefully configured Neovim for Lua from this little guide. Maybe in the next article, I'll set up LSP in Neovim. If you have any queries or feedback, please let me know. Thank you for reading.

Happy Viming :)
item.author: mr_destructive

item.title: TypeScript 数组类型 | TypeScript Array Type
item.description:
数组在 TypeScript 中的工作方式与在 Javascript 中的工作方式相同，唯一的区别是我们必须预先定义它们的类型。 数组，就其本质而言，是一个有序的数据列表。 定义Array的类型一开始可能会让人困惑，所以让我们看看它是如何工作的。

在 TypeScript 中定义数组类型

如果我们有一个数组，我们可以使用表示法type[]在 TypeScript 中定义它的类型。 例如，下面的变量arrayType应该是一个字符串数组。

 let arrayType : string [] = [ ' hello ' , ' there ' ]

类似地，一个数字数组可以这样定义：

 let myNumbers : number [] = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 ];

这也使任何未来的数组项都符合该类型。 例如，我们不能将string推送或添加到定义为类型number的数组的末尾：

 let myNumbers : number [] = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 ]; // Throws an error. myNumbers . push ( " string " );

我已经在这里写过关于 TypeScript 中的元组，但它们是一种数组类型，具有定义数量的指定类型的项。 例如，我们可以定义一个包含两个元素的数组，类型为string和number ，这将被称为tuple 。 这可以通过执行以下操作来实现：

 let myTuple :[ string , number ] = [ " hello " , 20 ];

元组可以是任意长度，您可以在此处了解有关如何定义元组的更多信息。

在 TypeScript 中将混合类型存储在数组中

有时，我们知道一个数组将由number或string元素组成，但我们不确定顺序是什么，甚至是多少。 因此，元组在这里并不是真正的正确选择。 相反，我们可以使用与以前相同的格式定义这样的数组，只让 TypeScript 知道它可以是多种类型。

例如，对于一个长度未知的数组，其中任何一项都可以是number或string ，我们可以编写以下代码：

 let type :( string | number )[] = [ ' hello ' , ' world ' , 20 , 40 , ' goodbye ' ];

在 TypeScript 中为数组使用通用类型格式

最后，还可以使用泛型类型定义格式在 TypeScript 中定义Array类型。 例如，一个数字Array可以这样定义：

 let type : Array < number > = [ 0 , 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 ]

或者，可以像这样定义项可以是string或number的数组：

 let type : Array < string | number > = [ ' hello ' , ' world ' , 20 , 40 , ' goodbye ' ];

结论

了解 TypeScript 中的数组类型是日常使用 TypeScript 的基础。 我希望你喜欢这个指南，如果你想要更多的 TypeScript 内容，你可以在这里找到它。

托梅克·波尼亚托维茨 - 一旦暂停，smpnjn 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，smpnjn 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，smpnjn 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 smpnjn 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Arrays work the same in TypeScript as they do in Javascript, the only difference being that we have to define their type upfront. Arrays, by their nature, are an ordered list of data. Defining an Arrays type can be confusing at first, so let's look at how it works.

Defining Array Types in TypeScript

If we have an array, we can define its type in TypeScript by using the notation type[]. For example, the below variable arrayType is expected to be an array of strings.

let arrayType:string[] = [ 'hello', 'there' ]

Similarly, an array of numbers could be defined like this:

let myNumbers:number[] = [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ];

This also conforms any future array items to that type. For example, we couldn't push or add a string to the end of an array defined as type number:

let myNumbers:number[] = [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ];
// Throws an error.
myNumbers.push("string");

Tuple Types in TypeScript

I've already written about tuples in TypeScript here, but they are a type of array with a defined number of items, of specified types. For example, we could define an array with two elements, of type string, and number, and this would be called a tuple. That could be achieved by doing something like this:

let myTuple:[ string, number ] = [ "hello", 20 ];

Tuples can be of any length, and you can learn more about how tuples are defined here.

Storing a Mixture of Types in an Array in TypeScript

Sometimes, we know an array is going to consist of either number or string elements, but we're not sure in what order, or even how many. As such, a tuple isn't really the right option here. Instead, we can define an array like this using the same format as before, only letting TypeScript know that it can be multiple types.

For example, for an array of unknown length where any item could be either a number or a string, we could write the following:

let type:(string | number)[] = [ 'hello', 'world', 20, 40, 'goodbye' ];

Using the Generic Type format for Arrays in TypeScript

Finally, it is also possible to use the generic type definition format for defining Array types in TypeScript. For example, an Array of numbers could be defined like so:

let type:Array<number> = [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 ]

Or, an array where items could be either a string or a number could be defined like so:

let type:Array<string | number> = [ 'hello', 'world', 20, 40, 'goodbye' ];

Conclusion

Understanding the array type in TypeScript is fundamental for using TypeScript day to day. I hope you've enjoyed this guide, and if you want more TypeScript content, you can find it all here.

Tomek Poniatowicz - Jul 6

Pinesucceed Pvt Ltd - Jun 23

Once suspended, smpnjn will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, smpnjn will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by smpnjn will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If smpnjn is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: smpnjn

item.title: 大型 CSV 文件的架构推断 | Schema Inference for Large CSV files
item.description:
一旦暂停，ramsescoraspe 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，ramsescoraspe 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，ramsescoraspe 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ramsescoraspe 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Once suspended, ramsescoraspe will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ramsescoraspe will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ramsescoraspe will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ramsescoraspe is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: ramsescoraspe

item.title: 如何创建一个简单的 Nginx Web 服务器？ | How to create a simple Nginx web server ?
item.description:
我的这篇文章的目标读者是想了解 Nginx、Docker、Containers、Node.js 的人。

学习目标

完成本文后，您将知道如何执行以下操作：

  • 创建一个简单的 node.js 应用程序
  • 创建和设置 nginx 配置
  • 为 node.js 和 nginx 创建 docker 镜像
  • 设置docker-compose.yml配置
  • 使用 docker compose 启动整个应用程序

什么是 Nginx？

在本文中，我们将使用 nginx 作为负载均衡器。

负载均衡

反向代理服务器可以充当“交通警察”，坐在后端服务器前面，以最大限度地提高速度和容量利用率的方式将客户端请求分发到一组服务器，同时确保没有一个服务器过载，这会降低性能. 如果服务器出现故障，负载均衡器会将流量重定向到剩余的在线服务器。

您可以在下面看到用于负载平衡的示例 nginx 配置：

 #ngnix.conf file http { upstream myapp1 { server srv1.example.com ; server srv2.example.com ; server srv3.example.com ; } server { listen 80 ; location / { proxy_pass http://myapp1 ; } } }

在这个例子中，我们监听 80 端口来处理请求。 对于每个请求，nginx 会将客户端路由到 srv1 到 srv3。 当没有特别配置负载均衡方式时，默认为round-robin。

您可以在此处查看其他负载平衡方法。

码头工人

它通过将应用程序隔离到图像中来工作。 该映像将由一个容器运行，其中包含操作系统和支持软件。 什么是码头集装箱？

当您使用 docker 将应用程序容器化时，您不必担心不同的环境。 因此，当您的应用程序在本地计算机上运行时，一旦您使用 docker 对其进行容器化，它也将在服务器上运行。 因为您指定使用哪些库，所以您的本地环境和服务器环境使用相同版本的库和操作系统。

用于节点应用程序的 Dockerfile 示例

FROM node:12 WORKDIR /home/node COPY . /home/node/ RUN npm install CMD npm run app

在 node.js 中创建一个简单的 http 服务器

在本文中，我们将创建一个简单的演示。 我们将使用HTTP库创建一个 HTTP Web 服务器。

以下是 node.js 中 HTTP Web 服务器的实现：

 const http = require ( " http " ); const defaultPort = 999 ; const portNumber = process . argv [ 2 ] || defaultPort ; const httpServer = http . createServer (( req , res ) => { res . statusCode = 200 ; res . end ( " this is port: " + portNumber ); }); httpServer . listen ( portNumber , " 0.0.0.0 " , () => { console . log ( " listening on port " , portNumber ); console . log ( httpServer . address ()); });

在这个实现之后，我们将启动两个具有不同端口的 Web 服务器。 您可以在下面看到示例命令行：

 node index.js 2222 #this will create a web server on port 2222 node index.js 3333 #this will create a web server on port 3333

启动两个 Web 服务器后，我们可以使用 Web 浏览器或使用curl对其进行测试。 您可以使用 curl 向 Web 服务器发出请求。 我们可以在下面的示例中看到 get 请求：

 curl -v localhost:2222 #make a get request on localhost:2222

回复：

 * Trying ::1... * TCP_NODELAY set * Connection failed * connect to ::1 port 2222 failed: Connection refused * Trying 127.0.0.1... * TCP_NODELAY set * Connected to localhost ( 127.0.0.1 ) port 2222 ( #0) > GET / HTTP/1.1 > Host: localhost:2222 > User-Agent: curl/7.64.1 > Accept: * / * > < HTTP/1.1 200 OK < Date: Sat, 09 Jul 2022 15:15:47 GMT < Connection: keep-alive < Keep-Alive: timeout = 5 < Content-Length: 18 < * Connection #0 to host localhost left intact this is port: 2222 * Closing connection 0

在这个演示中，我们将使用 nginx 作为负载均衡器在两个 Web 服务器之间进行路由。

这是实现：

 # nginx.conf file http { upstream all { server 192.168.1.36 : 3333 ; server 192.168.1.36 : 2222 ; } server { listen 8080 ; location / { proxy_pass http://all ; } } } events { }

我们必须构建这个镜像才能在 docker compose 中使用它。
您可以使用build命令构建 docker 映像。 这是一个例子

docker build -t nginxapp .

创建 nginx 镜像后，我们可以配置 docker-compose 文件。

这是docker-compose.yml的实现

version : " 3" services : nginxapp1 : image : nginxapp ports : - " 8080:8080"

您可以使用docker compose up启动应用程序，并使用docker compose down杀死和删除容器。

使用docker compose up启动 nginx 应用程序后。 您将在终端中看到示例输出：

 node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | /docker-entrypoint.sh: /docker-entrypoint.d/ is not empty, will attempt to perform configuration node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | /docker-entrypoint.sh: Looking for shell scripts in /docker-entrypoint.d/ node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | /docker-entrypoint.sh: Launching /docker-entrypoint.d/10-listen-on-ipv6-by-default.sh node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | 10-listen-on-ipv6-by-default.sh: info: Getting the checksum of /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | 10-listen-on-ipv6-by-default.sh: info: Enabled listen on IPv6 in /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | /docker-entrypoint.sh: Launching /docker-entrypoint.d/20-envsubst-on-templates.sh node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | /docker-entrypoint.sh: Launching /docker-entrypoint.d/30-tune-worker-processes.sh node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | /docker-entrypoint.sh: Configuration complete ; ready for start up

这意味着我们启动了 nginx 服务器，所以现在我们可以使用 curl 对其进行测试。

 ❯ curl localhost:8080 this is port: 3333% ❯ curl localhost:8080 this is port: 2222

您可以在此示例输出中看到，当我们向localhost:8080发出请求时，每个请求都使用循环算法发送到不同的 HTTP Web 服务器。



原文:

I've aimed this article at people who want to learn about Nginx, Docker, Containers, Node.js.

Learning Objectives

After completing this article, you will know how to do the following:

  • Create a simple node.js application
  • Create and setup nginx configuration
  • Create docker images for node.js and nginx
  • Setup docker-compose.yml configuration
  • Use docker compose to start entire application

What is Nginx ?

Nginx is a web server that can also be used as a reverse proxy, load balancer, mail proxy and HTTP cache.

In this article, we will use nginx as a load balancer.

Load balancing 

A reverse proxy server can act as a “traffic cop,” sitting in front of your backend servers and distributing client requests across a group of servers in a manner that maximizes speed and capacity utilization while ensuring no one server is overloaded, which can degrade performance. If a server goes down, the load balancer redirects traffic to the remaining online servers.

You can see an example nginx configuration for load balancing below:

#ngnix.conf file
http { upstream myapp1 { server srv1.example.com; server srv2.example.com; server srv3.example.com; } server { listen 80; location / { proxy_pass http://myapp1; } }
}

In this example, we are listening port 80 to handle requests. For each request, nginx will route the client to srv1 to srv3. When the load balancing method is not specifically configured, it defaults to round-robin.

You can check the other load balancing methods here.

Docker

Docker is a platform for deploying applications. It works by isolating the application into an image. The image will be run by a container, where it contains the operating system and supporting software. (source)

What is a docker container ?

A Docker container image is a lightweight, standalone, executable package of software that includes everything needed to run an application: code, runtime, system tools, system libraries and settings.

When you containerize an application using docker, you don't have to worry about different environments. So when your application runs on your local machine, once you containerize it using docker it will run on a server too. Because you specify which libraries used, so your local environment and a server environment use the same version of libraries and operating system.

An example for Dockerfile for a node application 

FROM node:12
WORKDIR /home/node
COPY . /home/node/
RUN npm install
CMD npm run app

Creating a simple http server in node.js

In this article, we will create a simple demo. We will create a HTTP web server using HTTP library.

Here is the implementation for HTTP web server in node.js:

const http = require("http"); const defaultPort = 999;
const portNumber = process.argv[2] || defaultPort; const httpServer = http.createServer((req, res) => { res.statusCode = 200; res.end("this is port: " + portNumber);
}); httpServer.listen(portNumber, "0.0.0.0", () => { console.log("listening on port ", portNumber); console.log(httpServer.address());
});

After this implementation, we will start two web servers with different ports. You can see the example command line below:

node index.js 2222 #this will create a web server on port 2222
node index.js 3333 #this will create a web server on port 3333

After starting two web servers, we can test it using web browser or using curl. You can use curl to make a request to a web server. We can see the get request in the example below:

curl -v localhost:2222 #make a get request on localhost:2222

Response:

* Trying ::1...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* Connection failed
* connect to ::1 port 2222 failed: Connection refused
* Trying 127.0.0.1...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 2222 (#0)
> GET / HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:2222
> User-Agent: curl/7.64.1
> Accept: */*
>
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Date: Sat, 09 Jul 2022 15:15:47 GMT
< Connection: keep-alive
< Keep-Alive: timeout=5
< Content-Length: 18
<
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact
this is port: 2222* Closing connection 0

Nginx configuration

In this demo, we will use nginx as a load balancer to route between two web servers.

Here is the implementation:

# nginx.conf file
http
{ upstream all { server 192.168.1.36:3333; server 192.168.1.36:2222; } server { listen 8080; location / { proxy_pass http://all; } }
} events
{
}

Dockerfile:

FROM nginx COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf

We have to build this image to use it in docker compose.
You can build a docker image using build command. Here is an example

docker build -t nginxapp .

After creating nginx image we can configure the dockore-compose file.

Here is the implementation for docker-compose.yml

version: "3" services: nginxapp1: image: nginxapp ports: - "8080:8080"

You can start the application using docker compose upand kill and remove the containers using docker compose down.

After starting the nginx application using docker compose up. You will see the example output in the terminal:

node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | /docker-entrypoint.sh: /docker-entrypoint.d/ is not empty, will attempt to perform configuration
node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | /docker-entrypoint.sh: Looking for shell scripts in /docker-entrypoint.d/
node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | /docker-entrypoint.sh: Launching /docker-entrypoint.d/10-listen-on-ipv6-by-default.sh
node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | 10-listen-on-ipv6-by-default.sh: info: Getting the checksum of /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | 10-listen-on-ipv6-by-default.sh: info: Enabled listen on IPv6 in /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | /docker-entrypoint.sh: Launching /docker-entrypoint.d/20-envsubst-on-templates.sh
node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | /docker-entrypoint.sh: Launching /docker-entrypoint.d/30-tune-worker-processes.sh
node-nginx-docker-tut-lb-1 | /docker-entrypoint.sh: Configuration complete; ready for start up

This means we started the nginx server, so now we can test it using curl.

❯ curl localhost:8080 
this is port: 3333%
❯ curl localhost:8080
this is port: 2222

You can see in this example output when we make a request to the localhost:8080 each request goes to the different HTTP web server using round-robin algorithm.
item.author: eminv_en

item.title: 重构 Redux - 一个聪明的方法 | Refactoring Redux - A clever way
item.description:
发表于此外，Redux 是一个简单的函数式编程范例的一个很好的例子，我们使用纯函数进行数据突变。 或者我们可以说，通过使用函数式编程，我们可以更加可预测并且最终减少系统中的错误。

它与 React.js 一起呈指数级流行。 但是，它可以与任何应用程序一起使用，并且独立于任何前端技术。 让我们讨论一下 Redux 的实际实现。

混乱从这里开始随着我们的产品经理用新功能丰富应用程序，我们的商店因此受到最大的影响。 没有。 我们的对象潜入嵌套的深层，嵌套对象的更新变得很痛苦，现在我们的 Clean reducer 变成了一个大泥球。

在寻找解决方案时，我最终发现了一些关于 redux 重构的技巧

  1. 在 switch case 中使用函数返回新状态
  2. 减速器内的扁平状态形状
  3. 使用combineReducer 组合多个减速器

让我们讨论一下它们是否真的可以帮助我们重构

  1. 我们可以导入纯函数并在我们的 reducer 中使用它们，但我们的 switch case 数量仍然是完整的。 这种方法最大的问题是它会影响可读性，因为现在我们的状态和状态突变逻辑位于单独的文件中。
    下一个是什么？？？？？

  2. 平面状态形状：在设计和创建减速器状态时必须牢记这一点。 更少的嵌套级别将导致更清洁的存储。 但是，如果我们的 reducer 已经搞砸了更深层次的对象和动作来改变这些对象呢？ 改变结构几乎是不可能的，因为它会影响所有连接的组件。
    组合减速器怎么样？？？

  3. 组合 Reducers ：这是将多个 reducer 组合在该州的一个键名下的好方法。 此函数接受多个减速器作为参数。 但这对于现有的混乱 reducer 也不是可行的方法，因为我们不能一次将所有东西都移动到多个 reducer 中。 我们需要一个循序渐进的方法来实现这一目标。 或者也许我们只想将状态的某个部分移动到单独的减速器中，而我们的状态的其余部分应该是完整的。 大多数情况下都会发生这种情况。 一个combineReducers示例如下所示

const vehicleReducer = combineReducers ({ twoWheelers , fourWheelers : combineReducers ( cars , suv , minitrucks ) }) const store = createStore ( vehicleReducer )

在上面的例子中，状态看起来像

{ twoWheelers : {}, fourWheelers : { cars : {}, suv : {}, minitrucks : {} }

应用挑战：在实际应用中，我们可能会深度嵌套弄乱下面这样的结构，并且其中的形​​状无法更改，或者我们必须花费大量时间重新映射组件。

 todoActivity : { documents : {...}, activityDetails : {...}, selectedTab : " TODO " , selectedActivities : [{}, {}], . . . }

这不能在todoActivity键下以更简洁的方式作为combinereducers进行组合。 所以，我认为，我可以为activityDetails创建单独的reducer，其余的键只能留在这个现有的reducer 中。 任何清理这种程度的烂摊子的方法，我都必须逐渐采取行动。 但是怎么办？？？

 export default newCleanerReducer ( state , action ) => ({ ... todoActivityReducer ( state , action ), activityDetails : activityReducer ( state ? state . activityDetails : undefined , action ) // lodash get can also be used instead of trenary operator });

如您所见，我创建了一个小型化简器，它传播当前状态并为其添加了activityDetails的activityReducer 。 这样，所有与activityDetails相关的混乱都可以移动到具有自己的类型、操作和reducer 的单独文件中。 这在我的activityReducer中给了我很大的灵活性。

  • 我可以在这里坚持关注点分离。
  • 这个减速器内部的状态形状变得比单个减速器中的更平坦。
  • 现在我可以将 switch case 分解为文件中的多个可重用函数（为了更好的可读性）

我们还可以使用 lodash 函数将其编写为无点风格，最终更具可读性。

我很想讨论更多关于这些主题的更清晰的代码。 您可以在下面留下您的评论并显示一些❤️拜托！

我可以再次以这句话结束：
一旦暂停，ashishsurana 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，ashishsurana 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，ashishsurana 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ashishsurana 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Ashish Surana

Posted on Jul 9

Redux is a A predictable state container for JavaScript apps.

Also, Redux is a nice example of a simple functional programming paradigm where we use pure functions for data mutation. Or we can say, by using functional programming we can be more predictable and eventually fewer errors in our system.

It became popular exponentially along with the React.js. However, It can be used with any application and it is independent of Any front end technology. Let's discuss the practical implementation of Redux.

Clutter starts from here As our Product managers enrich application with new features, our store experiences the maximum impact due to this. Length of Reducers grows linearly along with the source code. The no. of cases inside the switch blocks keep on increasing and in many cases reducer file length crosses 700, 900 lines. Our objects dive in the deep level of nesting, Updation of nested objects become a pain, And now Our Clean reducer became a big ball of mud.

While Looking for solutions, I end up finding few tips for redux refactoring

  1. Using functions to return new state inside switch cases
  2. Flat State shape inside a reducer
  3. using combineReducer to club multiple reducers

Let's discuss if they can really help us in refactoring

  1. Using functions: This can be a great way of clean code. We can import pure functions and use them in our reducers, but still, our number of switch cases are intact. The biggest problem with this approach is it can affect readability because now our state and state mutation logic lies in separate files.
    so what's next?????

  2. Flat State shape: This must be kept in mind while designing and creating reducer states. Fewer levels of nesting will lead to a cleaner store. But what if our reducer is already messed up with a deep level of objects and actions to mutuate those objects??? Changing structure is almost next to impossible because it will affect all connected components.
    How about combining Reducers???

  3. Combine Reducers: This is a great way to club multiple reducers under a single key name in the state. this function accepts multiple reducers as parameters. But this is also not a feasible approach for existing messed up reducers, because we cannot move everything into multiple reducers at once. We need a step by step approach for achieving this. Or maybe we only want to move a certain part of the state into the separate reducer and the rest of our state should be intact. This happens in most of the cases. A combineReducers example can be seen below

const vehicleReducer = combineReducers({ twoWheelers, fourWheelers: combineReducers(cars, suv, minitrucks)
})
const store = createStore(vehicleReducer)

In the above example, the state will look like

{
twoWheelers: {},
fourWheelers: { cars: {}, suv: {}, minitrucks: {}
}

Application Challenges: In real applications we might have deep nested messed up a structure like below one and of which, the shape cannot be changed or we have to spend a lot of time in mapping components again.

todoActivity: { documents: {...}, activityDetails: {...}, selectedTab: "TODO", selectedActivities: [{}, {}], . . .
}

This cannot be clubbed under todoActivity key as a combinereducers in a cleaner way. The todoActivity is having more than 15 keys already, many of them are deeply nested. So, I think, I can create separate reducer for activityDetails and the rest of the keys can stay inside this existing reducer only. Any way to clean this level of mess, I have to move gradually. But how???

export default newCleanerReducer(state, action) => ({ ...todoActivityReducer(state, action), activityDetails: activityReducer(state ? state.activityDetails : undefined, action)
// lodash get can also be used instead of trenary operator
});

As you can see, I have created a small reducer that spreads the current state and add activityDetails for its activityReducer. By this, all clutter related to activityDetails can be moved to separate files with its own types, action, and reducers. This gave me a lot of flexibility within my activityReducer.

  • I can adhere to the Separation of Concerns here.
  • State shape inside this reducer became flatter than what it was in a single reducer.
  • Now I can break switch cases into multiple reusable functions within the file (for better readability)

We can also use lodash functions to write it in point free style and more readable eventually.

I would love to discuss more about such topics for cleaner code. You can drop your comments below and show some ❤️ Please!

I can again conclude with this quote:
"Clean code always looks like it was written by someone who cares. - Robert C. Martin"

Once suspended, ashishsurana will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ashishsurana will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ashishsurana will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ashishsurana is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: ashishsurana

item.title: 日常活动 | Daily activities
item.description:
一旦暂停，thedzigner 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，thedzigner 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，thedzigner 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 thedzigner 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Once suspended, thedzigner will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, thedzigner will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by thedzigner will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If thedzigner is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 如果您没有动力加入初创公司，请阅读此内容 | Read this if you don't feel motivated joining a Start Up
item.description:
发表于多少天你会生活在对你工作的公司（初创公司）的恐惧和不安全感中，是时候崛起并开始培育你的公司成为第一个采取主动的人成为战士帮助你的公司到达目的地谁如果你不能，否则可以这样做，如果你认为有失败的机会，那么也有成功的机会，因为概率是 0.5 双方放弃 0.5 的消极性，让我们专注于事物的 0.5 积极的一面。 你的公司的旅程就像一场战争，你的公司是一支军队，你是那支军队中的战士，你有责任打这场战争而不是逃离战场，你只有两个选择 1. 胜利和 2 . 学习，你很幸运能加入创业，因为你就像一个有领导能力的军队长，你不再是一个普通的士兵，让这个创业之旅成为史诗，这样我们可以激励我们的年轻一代让这个旅程开始吧冒险，这样我们就可以像童话一样向我们的年轻一代讲述
让我们以我们的运营商决策来激励他人，不要在热衷于加入创业公司的年轻人心中制造恐慌

这篇文章背后的动机是我们中的许多人加入创业公司，因为我们希望我们的工作具有挑战性，我们希望为我们获得的技能伸张正义，我们希望创造一种对其他人产生更大影响的产品，但我们的社会创造了一个我们担心初创公司风险很大，并且可以安全地加入成熟的合作公司，无论是加入初创公司还是加入大型合作公司，我们都陷入了两难境地。 即使我们加入了一家初创公司，我们也会开始怀疑自己的决定是否正确以上结论

How many days you would live in the fear and feel insecure about the company (startup) which you work, its time to rise up and start to nurture your company be the first one to take initiative be a warrior help you company reaching its destination who else can do it if you can't, if you think there is a chance of failure then there is also a chance for success since the probability is 0.5 each sides let go of the 0.5 negativity lets focus on the 0.5 positive side of things. the journey of your company is like a war and your company is a army and you are a warrior in that army its your responsibility to to fight that war not to run away from the battle field, you have only two choices 1. victory and 2. learning, you are lucky to join a startup because you are like the army chief who has the power to lead and you are no more a ordinary soldier lets make this startup journey a epic so that we could motivate our younger generation lets make this journey adventurous so that we could narrate it like a fairy tale to our younger generation
lets motivate others with our carrier decision, lets not create panic in the hearts of young people who are passionate about joining a startup

the motivation behind this post was many of us join startup's because we wanted our job to be challenging and we wanted to do justice for the skills we acquired and we wanted to create a product that has a greater impact on others but our society has created a fear within us that startups are very risky and its safe joining a well established cooperate and we fall into this dilemma whether to join a startup or and big cooperate company. even if we join a startup we start doubting our self whether the decision i have taken is correct or not we compare our jobs with those of our fiends and feel insecure even i went through this phase of suffering but through self motivation i came up with the above conclusion

Best Books for Algorithms and Data Structures in programming:

1) Cracking The Coding Interview: 189 Programming Questions and Solutions - 6th Edition - Gayle Laakmann McDowell

2) Algorithms in A Nutshell: : A Practical Guide, Second Edition - George Heineman, Gary Pollice, Stanley Selkow

3) Thomas H. Cormen - Algorithms Unlocked (The MIT Press)

4) Adnan Aziz and Amit Prakash - Algorithms For Interviews: A Problem Solving Approach

5) Narasimha Karumanchi - Data Structures And Algorithms Made Easy: Data Structures And Algorithmic Puzzles

6) Introduction To Algorithms 3rd Edition - Thomas H Cormen, Charles E Leiserson, Ronald L Rivest, Clifford Stein

7) Dynamic Programming for Coding Interviews - Meenakshi and Kamal Rawat

8) Data Structures and Algorithms. Aho, Ullman & Hopcroft

9) Algorithms by Robert Sedgewick & Kevin Wayne

10) Algorithm Design by Kleinberg & Tardos

11) The Design and Analysis of Algorithms - Anany Levitin

12) The Programming Pearls (2nd Edition) by Jon Bentley

13) The Algorithm Design Manual by Steve S. Skiena

14) Beautiful Code: Leading Programmers Explain How They Think (Theory in Practice (O'Reilly))

15) Elements of Programming Interviews: The Insiders' Guide 2nd Edition by Adnan Aziz, Tsung-Hsien Lee, Amit Prakash

item.title: javascript 中的闭包 (🔖) | Closures in javascript (🔖)
item.description:
这是一个非常简单的话题，也非常重要。 很多输出问题都来自这个问题。

所以闭包只不过是功能+它是外部环境。

让我们举一个例子来分解这个语句：

 function hi(){ var k="I am great!" function reply(){ console.log(k); } k="Long time no see" reply(); return reply; } var ans=hi(); console.log(ans)

在这种情况下， function reply()与它的周围形成一个闭包，在这种情况下是function hi() 。 因此，当尝试访问内部的 k 时，它会获取值。 此外，如您所见，我们更新了k的值，回复函数也获取了该值。

一旦返回， reply()在整个执行过程中仍然保持它的环境。 这是因为它返回一个闭包。

正如上一篇博客中提到的，这解释了词法作用域。 一个函数不仅可以访问它的父作用域，还可以访问它的父作用域等等。 这种访问在词法函数之外也是有效的。

如果您是 React 开发人员，当有人询问状态是否同步时，这是一个非常常见的问题。 建议通过这个！
一旦暂停，aishanipach 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，aishanipach 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，aishanipach 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 aishanipach 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

This is a very easy topic and quite important too. A lot of output questions are framed out of this one.

So a closure is nothing but function + it's outside environment.

Let's take an example to break this statement down:

function hi(){
   var k="I am great!"

    function reply(){
        console.log(k);
    }

    k="Long time no see"
    reply();
    return reply;

}
var ans=hi();
console.log(ans)

In this case function reply() forms a closure with it's surrounding, function hi() in this case. So, when trying to access k inside, it gets the value. Moreover, as you can see we update value of k, which the reply function pick ups too.

Once returned, the reply() still holds on to it's environment throughout the execution. This is because it returns a closure.

As mentioned in the last blog, this explains the lexical scope. A function can not only access it's parent scope but also it's parent's parent scope and so on. This access is also valid outside the lexical function.

If you are a react developer, it is a very common question when someone asks if states are synchronous. Would recommend to go through this!
⭐Async and setState

Gauthier Cassany - Jun 27

Darragh O'Riordan - Jun 23

item.title: CTF 平台并不完全是仙境 | CTF platforms are not exactly wonderlands
item.description:
我一直在为 CTF 测试各种平台。 以下是一些保持安全的建议。

许多用户已经是黑客......

这是我很少在有关 CTF 的博客文章中看到的内容，但我不会 100% 信任黑客平台。

不要误会我的意思。 我当然不会指责任何平台做奇怪的事情，但这些工具不仅适用于有抱负的黑客。 虽然其中一些非常适合初学者（您可以从 0 知识开始），但熟练且经验丰富的黑客使用它们来竞争等等。

我为什么要告诉你？

根据您的配置，您可能会将自己置于危险之中。 例如，如果您将笔记本电脑与虚拟机一起使用并启用双向同步，则可能存在一些风险。 如果您从办公室连接，则更加危险。

请不要在未经许可和适当环境（例如分段、沙盒）的情况下安装 Kali Linux 和 hacking box。

此外，使用盒子打开通信通道以启用反向外壳、下载黑客工具包或泄露信息的情况并不少见。 有些玩家可能没有意识到netcat或telnet等基本命令可能很容易使用，但它们不会加密任何东西。

事实上，大多数初学者平台都会让你做几乎任何你想做的事情，包括最马虎的攻击。 目标是练习，有时会失败并了解原因，然后再试一次^^。

实际条件下需要的 CTF 中的 5 个可选操作

  • 没有必要掩盖你的踪迹
  • 您不必使用代理链或屏蔽您的真实 IP
  • 您不必避开可以发现异常活动的检测工具
  • 很少需要混淆，您可以像没有扫描仪一样直接攻击
  • 您不是匿名的，该平台可以跟踪您的进度，甚至公开显示您的活动

安全开始

我认为重要的是不要盲目，尤其是当你是初学者时。 并非所有平台对用户的隐私和安全都有相同的标准。

再说一次，我不会在这里责怪任何人。 大多数时候，免费获得这些知识真是太棒了。 但是，执行您不完全理解的脚本也很容易，或者您可能会在您的机器上打开危险端口（如端口 80），只是为了获得一个标志和一些随机分数，这不一定反映您的真实水平，更多的是一个你进步的指标。

我会推荐以下内容：

  • 至少掩盖你的真实IP
  • 不要仅仅因为反恶意软件与您的本地设置冲突就禁用它
  • 不要为了方便和游戏而牺牲安全
  • 不要打开你的机器超过严格必要的（注意你的端口）

包起来

尽管存在这些缺点，但黑客平台仍然是学习技能的好地方。 人们花时间建立游戏化体验，以减轻痛苦并加快学习曲线。

但是，要小心并保持安全。 对我来说，这是学习路径的一部分。



原文:

I've been testing various platforms for CTFs. Here are some advices to stay safe.

Many users are already hackers...

It's something I rarely see in blog posts about CTFs, but I would not trust hacking platforms 100%.

Don't get me wrong. I'm certainly not accusing any platform of doing weird things, but these tools are not only meant for aspiring hackers. While some of them are great for complete beginners (you can start with 0 knowledge), skilled and experienced hackers use them to compete, among other things.

Why am I telling you that?

Depending on your configuration, you might put yourself at risk. For example, if you use your laptop with virtual machines and you enable bidirectional synchronizations, there could be some risks. It's even more dangerous if you connect from work office.

Please do not install Kali Linux and hacking boxes without permission and an appropriate environment (e.g., segmentation, sandboxing).

In addition, it's not uncommon to open communication channels with boxes to enable reverse shells, download hacking kits, or exfiltrate information. Some players might not realize that basic commands such as netcat or telnet may be easy to use, but they don't encrypt anything.

In fact, most platforms for beginners will let you do almost whatever you want, including the most sloppy attacks. The goal is to practice, fail sometimes and understand why, then try again ^^.

5 optional actions in CTFs that would be required in real conditions

  • there's no need to cover your tracks
  • you don't have to use proxy chains or mask your real IP
  • you don't have to evade detection tools that can spot anomalous activity
  • obfuscation is rarely required, you can attack directly like there's no scanner
  • you're not anonymous, the platform can track your progression and even display your activity publicly

Getting started safely

I think it's important not to go blindly, especially when you're a beginner. Not all platforms have the same standards regarding users' privacy and safety.

Again, I won't blame anyone here. It's amazing to get such knowledge, most of the time, for free. However, it's also easy to execute scripts you don't fully understand or your may leave dangerous ports open on your machine (like port 80) just to get a flag and some random score, which does not necessarily reflect your real level and is more an indicator of your progression.

I would recommend the following:

  • at least mask your real IP
  • don't disable anti-malware software just because it conflicts with your local setup
  • don't sacrifice safety over convenience and gaming
  • don't open your machine more than strictly necessary (watch your ports)

Wrap up

Despite these drawbacks, hacking platforms are still amazing places to learn your craft. People give their time to build gamified experiences to ease the pain and accelerate the learning curve.

However, be cautious and stay safe. It's part of the learning path, to me.
